items.back: Operable two-way radio AND operable radar beacon transponder with automatic altitude reporting capability. More stringent than Class C (radio only) or TRSA (voluntary participation).
VFR aircraft must obtain an ATC CLEARANCE to operate in Class B airspace. Distinct from Class C (two-way radio only).

Standard IFR services to IFR aircraft. VFR aircraft must be SEPARATED from VFR/IFR aircraft >19,000 lbs AND turbojets. Traffic advisories and safety alerts for all. Merging target procedures per 5-1-4.
MANDATORY traffic advisories and safety alerts between ALL aircraft (not workload-permitting). Apply merging target procedures per 5-1-4.
VFR helicopters/powered-lift need NOT be separated from VFR or IFR helicopters/powered-lift. BUT: traffic advisories and safety alerts still mandatory.
